title: Replicate API tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Replicate API token detected in source code. Replicate provides a cloud platform for running machine learning models, including Stable Diffusion, LLaMA, DALL-E, and thousands of other AI models. These API tokens authenticate programmatic access to Replicate's API, granting the ability to run models, retrieve predictions, access model outputs, manage deployments, and consume API credits. Exposing this token allows attackers to run expensive AI models using your credits leading to significant financial charges, execute arbitrary machine learning inference operations, access and exfiltrate model outputs including potentially sensitive generated content, abuse your account for cryptocurrency mining or other computational workloads, deplete your API quota causing service disruption to legitimate applications, access prediction history and model usage patterns revealing business intelligence, and potentially violate terms of service resulting in account suspension. IMMEDIATELY revoke this token from your Replicate account at https://replicate.com/account/api-tokens, generate a new replacement token with appropriate permissions, audit recent API usage in your Replicate dashboard for unauthorized model runs or suspicious activity patterns, review billing statements for unexpected charges from unauthorized usage, check prediction logs and model execution history for signs of abuse, consider implementing IP whitelisting or rate limiting if available, store tokens securely using environment variables, secret management systems (AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault, GCP Secret Manager), or encrypted configuration files (never commit tokens to version control or expose in client-side code), implement proper access controls and least privilege principles for token distribution, rotate tokens regularly as a security best practice (every 90 days recommended), monitor API usage patterns and set up billing alerts to detect anomalous activity, use separate tokens for different environments (development, staging, production) to limit blast radius of compromise, and implement rate limiting and quota management in your applications to prevent abuse.
